safety_properties.name: PermissionBeforePower
safety_properties.description: No actuation without valid release token

safety_properties.name: EvidenceBeforeToken
safety_properties.description: Token issued only after evidence appended

safety_properties.name: TokenRequiredForActuation
safety_properties.description: Actuation gate rejects without valid token

safety_properties.name: ModeDecisionConsistency
safety_properties.description: Mode and decision are always consistent

safety_properties.name: FaultIsLatched
safety_properties.description: Once in FAULT, stays in FAULT (two sub-properties)

safety_properties.name: NoDirectInertToRun
safety_properties.description: No single transition from INERT to RUN

safety_properties.name: BootPromotionValid
safety_properties.description: BOOT promotion only with valid conditions

safety_properties.name: ChainIntegrityInvariant
safety_properties.description: Evidence chain hash-links are unbroken (three sub-properties)

liveness_properties.name: EventuallyRun
liveness_properties.description: System eventually reaches RUN under persistent good conditions

liveness_properties.name: RunExitsOnLowSafety
liveness_properties.description: RUN exits when safety confidence drops below threshold

liveness_properties.name: BootEventuallyCompletes
liveness_properties.description: BOOT phase completes within bounded time

liveness_properties.name: PipelineReturnsToIdle
liveness_properties.description: Request pipeline always returns to IDLE

layers.domain: System architecture — bounded exhaustive model checking
Rust implementation correctness — bounded model checking
key_engineering_decisions: Replaced dynamic allocation (Vec, String, format!) with fixed-size [u8; 32] XOR operations for CBMC compatibility
Built AbstractModeManager with KappaRegion enum to eliminate IEEE 754 f64 bit-blasting from SAT solving
#[cfg(kani)] crypto stubs for ring::digest and ring::hmac FFI boundaries

layers.domain: Unbounded proof — all possible inputs via SMT
key_properties: Hash modeled as uninterpreted function (Z3 oracle) — proofs hold for ANY hash algorithm
Same AbstractModeManager + KappaRegion abstraction as Kani layer (bisimulation relation)
All quantifiers are universal (forall) — no existential witnesses required

warnings.message: hash_oracle needs uninterp annotation in future Verus versions

layers.domain: Runtime assertion checking — live production traffic

results.description: Python and Rust implementations produce byte-identical outputs for all test vectors

layers.domain: Interactive theorem proving — machine-checked mathematical proof

results.proof_strategy: Induction on Reachable relation with case analysis on Next

safety_SP1_SP12.name: SP6
safety_SP1_SP12.property: ModeDecisionConsistency — ALLOW/CLAMP only in RUN mode

safety_SP1_SP12.name: SP7
safety_SP1_SP12.property: FaultIsLatched — modePrev=FAULT implies mode is FAULT or INERT

safety_SP1_SP12.name: SP9
safety_SP1_SP12.property: BootPromotionValid — RUN from BOOT requires consecutiveOk >= nPromote

safety_SP1_SP12.name: SP10
safety_SP1_SP12.property: BootTimeoutBounded — BOOT duration bounded by bT + dT + 1

safety_SP1_SP12.name: SP11
safety_SP1_SP12.property: DenyZeroActuation — DENY or HALT implies zero actuation

liveness.name: pipeline_progress
liveness.property: Every non-idle phase has a strictly closer successor

liveness.name: LP4_PipelineCompletes
liveness.property: Pipeline returns to IDLE in at most 6 steps (well-founded induction on phaseDist)

liveness.name: LP3_BootExits
liveness.property: BOOT exits when timeout exceeded and dwell satisfied

liveness.name: LP2_RunExits
liveness.property: RUN exits when kappaRegion drops to belowOff

liveness.name: LP1_BootProgress
liveness.property: BOOT accumulates toward RUN or promotes when consecutiveOk >= nPromote

liveness.name: LP1_InertToBoot
liveness.property: INERT transitions to BOOT under good conditions

key_properties: Faithful translation of KevrosEnforcementKernel.tla (716 lines TLA+ -> 760 lines Lean 4)
KappaRegion abstraction eliminates IEEE 754 — same as Kani/Verus layers
All proofs by induction on Reachable with case analysis on Next — no sorry, no axioms
Strengthened invariant with 27 conjuncts enables mechanical inductive step
Hash function modeled as uninterpreted (chainIntact : Bool) — proofs hold for any hash
Liveness encoded as bounded progress via well-founded phaseDist variant

relationship_to_other_layers.layer_1_TLA: Lean 4 spec is a faithful 1:1 translation of the TLA+ spec; same state space, same Next relation, same safety invariants
relationship_to_other_layers.layer_2_Kani: Shares KappaRegion abstraction and safety property numbering (SP1-SP12)
relationship_to_other_layers.layer_3_Verus: Complementary — Verus proves Rust implementation; Lean 4 proves mathematical model. Both use induction on reachability.

comparison.note: No published organization has demonstrated six-layer formal verification on a single product

aws.applied_to: Different tools on different products (DynamoDB, S3, Firecracker, Encryption SDK)

microsoft.applied_to: Different tools on different products (Cosmos DB, Windows, research case studies)

kevros.applied_to: All six tools on ONE product (Kevros Enforcement Kernel)
